reviews.author: Bảo Quang Nguyễn
reviews.text: Mằng Lăng Church is a historic religious site in my hometown of Phú Yên and the place where the earliest Vietnamese script book is preserved. Located about 35 km north of Tuy Hòa City, Mằng Lăng Church is the oldest Roman Catholic church in Phú Yên and one of the oldest in Vietnam. It was built in 1892 by French missionary Father Joseph de La Cassagne. The name “Mằng Lăng” comes from the many Lagerstroemia (purple trumpet) trees that once grew in this area. As a Catholic church, the site features Gothic architectural lines along with impressive and colorful cathedral domes. Mằng Lăng Church is also the birthplace of Blessed Andrew of Phú Yên, who was martyred in 1644 and later declared the Patron of Youth. Today, the church remains an important spiritual and community site for local believers. One of the most remarkable aspects of Mằng Lăng Church is that it preserves the first Vietnamese script book, Phép Giảng Tám Ngày (Catechismus in Octo Dies). The book was written by missionary Alexandre de Rhodes (local residents call him "cha Đắc Lộ") and printed in Rome in 1651. Visitors can view the original book in person, as it is carefully displayed in a glass cabinet inside the church. During your visit, you may also witness various religious activities and ceremonies conducted by local believers. I highly recommend visiting Mằng Lăng Church along with nearby attractions such as Gành Đá Đĩa (Reef of Stone Plates), Gành Đèn Lighthouse, and Ô Loan Lagoon, as they are all located within the same area.

reviews.author: PIERRE PHU TIEN NGUYEN
reviews.text: This old place is called Little Seminary in Qui Nhon Binh Dinh Vietnam. Lots of history, lots of memories. It has been standing in the place throughout the wars and weather. It is a very beautiful place to visit.

reviews.author: PL Lau
reviews.text: Mằng Lăng Church is a must-visit for history buffs and those seeking a serene escape. The church's unique architecture, a blend of European and Vietnamese styles, is a testament to the country's rich cultural heritage. The architecture is breathtaking, with intricate details and a sense of grandeur that is both awe-inspiring and peaceful. Its location amidst lush greenery adds to its charm. The peaceful atmosphere and the sound of nature create a perfect ambiance for reflection. While the interior is as impressive as the exterior, the church's historical significance is what truly sets it apart. Nhà Thờ Mằng Lăng, located in Phú Yên, Vietnam, is a stunning historic Catholic church that is well worth a visit.

reviews.author: Hye Huang
reviews.text: The church has a small area, but for me, as a person who likes small corners to take pictures, it's more than enough, it's really beautiful, I feel satisfied when I chose to come here! Especially quite close to Da Di, so it should be in your schedule

reviews.author: Hung Hoang Van
reviews.text: A beautiful church with some historical Background. The place is kept in good shape and worth a visit.

reviews.author: Dave Drifter
reviews.text: Beautiful old church with lots of character. Don’t miss the grotto and the chapel inside along with the historic text.

reviews.author: An Duong
reviews.text: Very nice ancient Catholic Church. The hair of saint André Phu Yen kept here.

reviews.author: Chloe Tran
reviews.text: Peaceful church, ancient ❤️ Take time to go to this place when you're in Phu Yen city centre

reviews.author: Minh Nguyen
reviews.text: This church archives the first prints in the Vietnamese alphabet and marks one of the earliest influences of Christianity in Vietnam.

reviews.author: Dr. Stephen Bui
reviews.text: Visited the church and learned the history of modern Vietnamese language with Latin roots. Alexandre de Rhodes (15 March 1593– 5 November 1660) was an Avignonese Jesuit missionary and lexicographer who had a lasting impact on Christianity in Vietnam. He wrote the Dictionarium Annamiticum Lusitanum et Latinum, the first trilingual Vietnamese-Portuguese-Latin dictionary, published in Rome, in 1651.

reviews.author: Kent Kuo
reviews.text: A mini Cathedral need to be maintained.

reviews.author: Ganesh
reviews.text: Nhà Thờ Mằng Lăng, a historic Catholic church, captivates with its timeless beauty. Tucked away in a serene village, the church's ancient architecture adds a unique charm to the surroundings. Visiting upon a friend's recommendation, I found the location to be tranquil and the structure to be a testament to the rich history of the area. It's a must-visit for those who appreciate the beauty of old churches and seek a peaceful atmosphere. No entry ticket. It's free, anyone can visit.

reviews.author: Aki
reviews.text: Ancient church out of nowhere, pretty nice i guess There're some nice spot for photo tho

reviews.author: Marko Koivisto
reviews.text: Beautiful old church (1892) and great place for some pics too 👌

reviews.author: Linh Tran
reviews.text: One of the oldest church in Vietnam.

reviews.author: Joe C
reviews.text: Beautiful architecture that is still preserved today. This beautiful Catholic Church is still active and we went here on a Sunday when service was done. Well worth a trip to tour the inside and witness a church that is still standing since built in the 1800s.

reviews.author: Alvin Lai
reviews.text: Amazing structure . Worth visiting. Remember to read the history of this building on the back of the church.

reviews.text: Nice church and Gothic style at Phu Yên province
